refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site-specific duty cycle context, making the requirement verifiable and traceable to operational constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct field trials under representative duty cycle conditions; collect cycle time data via onboard telemetry; compute p95 percentile; compare against CT_target; document site conditions and load profiles.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with minimal idle/transition time.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide responsive control inputs with latency ≤ acceptable_threshold to enable operator-driven cycle optimization.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall not introduce cycle time penalties beyond safety-critical hold points.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, segment durations) for KPI analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle powertrain, hydraulics, and control logic to minimize load-travel-dump cycle duration; implement predictive load sensing and adaptive gear selection.
DP-2.2: Design teleoperation network and control stack to minimize command latency and operator reaction time; use low-latency communication protocols and local autonomy fallback.
DP-2.3: Architect safety services to enforce safety constraints without blocking non-critical cycle operations; use parallel safety monitoring and conditional hold logic.
DP-2.4: Implement onboard telemetry collection and edge processing to capture cycle segment timestamps and transmit KPI summaries via network link.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Integrate optimized vehicle dynamics (powertrain, hydraulics, control), responsive teleoperation (low-latency command/feedback), non-blocking safety services (parallel monitoring), and continuous telemetry collection to achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time optimization is coupled to teleoperation latency (operator must perceive state and issue timely commands).
Safety services must not introduce blocking delays on non-critical operations (conditional hold logic required).
Telemetry collection must not consume vehicle compute/network resources that degrade control responsiveness.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via architectural separation: vehicle dynamics (Vehicle module), teleoperation (Teleoperation module), safety logic (Safety Services module), and telemetry (distributed across modules with aggregation in NGHE Solution). Decoupling strategy: use asynchronous telemetry, prioritize control over monitoring, and design safety holds to be orthogonal to cycle operations.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware constraints (powertrain, hydraulics, actuation bandwidth).
Assumption: Teleoperation network latency can be kept below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip).
Assumption: Safety-critical holds (e.g., stability checks, proximity alerts) occur at predictable points in the cycle and do not cascade.
Risk: If vehicle hardware is undersized, cycle time target may be unachievable regardless of control optimization.
Risk: If network latency is high or variable, operator-driven optimization becomes difficult and cycle time variance increases.
Risk: If safety services introduce unpredictable delays, p95 cycle time may exceed target even if nominal cycle time is acceptable.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 to explicitly state latency budgets (e.g., teleoperation ≤ 200 ms, safety hold ≤ 5 s per cycle) and vehicle performance targets (e.g., load acceleration ≥ X m/s², travel speed ≥ Y km/h). Add risk mitigation: conduct early powertrain/network feasibility studies and define safety hold logic to minimize cycle impact.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time measurement, aggregation of telemetry, and reporting of p95 metrics to stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution collects cycle time data (segment timestamps, durations, operator actions) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules and transmits p95 KPI summaries and raw cycle logs via Telemetry interface to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definitions, CT_target updates, and operational mode commands from Site Manager via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety hold notifications and constraint updates from Safety Services module via SafetyAlert interface to inform cycle time analysis and operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to coordinate teleoperation latency, synchronize telemetry transmission, and manage bandwidth allocation between control and monitoring traffic.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI orchestration, measurement aggregation, and reporting. Cycle time is a system-level metric that spans Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; NGHE Solution integrates these concerns. Minimal diffusion: telemetry collection is distributed (Vehicle and Teleoperation report timestamps), but aggregation and KPI computation are centralized in NGHE Solution.

Vehicle owns the load-travel-dump cycle execution and can optimize its own segment durations (powertrain, hydraulics, control). However, Vehicle cannot own the full KPI because cycle time depends on teleoperation latency and safety holds, which are outside Vehicle scope. Diffusion risk: if Vehicle tries to own cycle time optimization without coordination with Teleoperation and Safety Services, it will duplicate logic and miss system-level constraints.

Teleoperation owns command latency and operator responsiveness, which directly impact cycle time. However, Teleoperation cannot own the full KPI because it does not control vehicle dynamics or safety constraints. Diffusion risk: if Teleoperation tries to optimize cycle time independently, it may conflict with Vehicle control logic or Safety Services constraints.

Safety Services owns safety-critical holds and constraints that affect cycle time. However, Safety Services should not own cycle time optimization because its primary goal is safety, not productivity. Diffusion risk: if Safety Services is tasked with minimizing cycle time impact, it may compromise safety logic or introduce conflicting constraints.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Cycle time KPI is a system-level metric that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) NGHE Solution can own telemetry aggregation and KPI reporting without duplicating logic in lower modules; (3) NGHE Solution can coordinate cycle time targets and constraints across modules via Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces; (4) Ownership in NGHE Solution avoids diffusion of cycle time optimization logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may lack visibility into Vehicle internal state (e.g., hydraulic pressure, gear selection) needed to diagnose cycle time bottlenecks. Mitigation: Vehicle must expose detailed telemetry (not just cycle timestamps) via Telemetry interface.

Risk: NGHE Solution may not have authority to enforce cycle time targets if Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services operate autonomously. Mitigation: Define clear coordination protocol (e.g., NGHE Solution sets CT_target via Tasking, modules acknowledge and report compliance via Telemetry).

Risk: Telemetry collection and KPI computation may introduce latency or resource contention that degrades control responsiveness. Mitigation: Use asynchronous telemetry collection, prioritize control traffic over monitoring, and implement edge processing in Vehicle and Teleoperation to reduce NGHE Solution compute load.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine IM assignment to specify: (1) Telemetry schema for cycle time data (segment names, timestamps, durations, operator actions, safety holds); (2) Tasking protocol for CT_target updates and duty cycle definitions; (3) SafetyAlert schema for safety hold notifications; (4) Network Link bandwidth allocation strategy to ensure control latency is not degraded by telemetry transmission. Add ownership responsibility: NGHE Solution shall compute and report p95 cycle time, identify bottlenecks (Vehicle vs. Teleoperation vs. Safety Services), and recommend optimization actions to Site Manager.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2 directly addresses FR-2 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target) by integrating vehicle optimization, teleoperation responsiveness, non-blocking safety services, and continuous telemetry. DP-2 is complete (covers all sub-FRs) and feasible (no fundamental physics or technology barriers). Coupling is manageable via architectural separation.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time optimization logic could diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if ownership boundaries are not clear. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns KPI aggregation and reporting; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective segment optimization and report telemetry to NGHE Solution.

Telemetry collection could become a bottleneck if not designed for asynchronous, low-overhead operation. Mitigation: Use edge processing in Vehicle and Teleoperation; NGHE Solution aggregates summaries, not raw data.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for cycle time KPI orchestration because it integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services and owns system-level metrics. Telemetry interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and avoid diffusion. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services remain focused on their respective concerns (dynamics, control, safety) and report telemetry to NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-2 is verifiable via field trials: collect cycle time data from NGHE Solution telemetry, compute p95 percentile, compare against CT_target. Verification requires representative duty cycle, site conditions, and operator profiles. Feasibility is high if Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services provide required telemetry.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns cycle time KPI measurement and reporting; Vehicle owns load-travel-dump execution; Teleoperation owns command latency; Safety Services owns safety-critical holds. Each module has a distinct responsibility and interface contract.

recommended_revisions: Refine Tasking interface to include CT_target, duty cycle definition, and operational mode (e.g., productivity-optimized vs. safety-conservative).
Refine Telemetry interface to include cycle segment timestamps, operator actions, safety hold events, and vehicle state (powertrain, hydraulics) for bottleneck diagnosis.
Refine SafetyAlert interface to include hold reason, duration, and impact on cycle time for NGHE Solution to correlate safety constraints with KPI variance.
Define Network Link bandwidth allocation strategy to ensure teleoperation latency is not degraded by telemetry transmission.
Add acceptance criteria: p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with telemetry data available for post-analysis.

decision.justification: FR-2 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (vehicle execution, teleoperation responsiveness, safety non-blocking, telemetry collection). Each sub-FR requires further refinement and DP selection at the next level. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM owner, but detailed design of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules requires decomposition of their respective sub-FRs. Proceed to next level with sub-FRs: FR-2.1 (vehicle cycle optimization), FR-2.2 (teleoperation latency), FR-2.3 (safety non-blocking), FR-2.4 (telemetry collection).
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR-2 is clear and verifiable, but it spans multiple architectural domains (vehicle dynamics, teleoperation control, safety logic, telemetry). DP-2 is a system-level integration strategy that requires decomposition into module-specific DPs. NGHE Solution is the correct IM owner for KPI orchestration, but Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules must be refined separately. Decomposition is necessary to allocate cycle time budgets, define interface contracts, and assign ownership within each module.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations with minimal idle/transition time, achieving segment durations ≤ target_load_time, target_travel_time, target_dump_time.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide responsive control inputs with command latency ≤ acceptable_latency and operator feedback latency ≤ acceptable_feedback_latency.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without introducing blocking delays on non-critical cycle operations; safety holds shall be conditional and time-bounded.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect cycle time telemetry (segment timestamps, durations, operator actions, safety events) and compute/report p95 KPI to stakeholders.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware constraints (powertrain, hydraulics, actuation bandwidth).
Teleoperation network latency can be kept below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip).
Safety-critical holds occur at predictable points in the cycle and do not cascade unpredictably.
Site conditions (terrain, load profiles, operator skill) are representative of agreed duty cycle.
Telemetry collection and KPI computation do not introduce latency or resource contention that degrades control responsiveness.
open_risks: Vehicle hardware may be undersized for CT_target; early feasibility study required.
Network latency may be high or variable, degrading operator-driven optimization; network design review required.
Safety services may introduce unpredictable delays; safety hold logic must be analyzed for cycle time impact.
Telemetry collection may become a bottleneck; edge processing and asynchronous design required.
Operator skill and duty cycle variability may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target; training and operational procedures required.
evidence_needed: Vehicle powertrain and hydraulics performance data (acceleration, speed, efficiency curves).
Teleoperation network latency measurements under representative site conditions.
Safety services hold logic and timing analysis (hold duration, frequency, impact on cycle).
Telemetry collection and processing performance (latency, throughput, resource utilization).
Field trial data: cycle time measurements under agreed duty cycle, p95 percentile, bottleneck analysis.
Operator feedback on control responsiveness and cycle time optimization strategies.
